parameters.name: group
parameters.label: Group
parameters.description: If there are input groups, specifying a group name in this field will cause this to act only upon the group specified. Accepts patterns, as described in .

parameters.name: method
parameters.label: Method
parameters.description: Specifies one of two fitting styles: approximation or interpolation. Each style has a number of parameters that are accessible from the respective folder. For more information see the Approximation an

parameters.name: type
parameters.label: Primitive Type
parameters.description: The output of the Fit is a NURBS or Bzier primitive. All input faces are fitted to Spline curves, and all input surfaces are fitted to spline surfaces. The resulting shapes are identical whether creat

parameters.name: surftype
parameters.label: Connectivity
parameters.description: This option is used to select the type of surface, when using a Mesh Type.

parameters.name: orderu
parameters.label: U Order
parameters.description: If the input is a face, this is the order of the Spline curve to be generated. If the input is a surface, this is the order of the fitted spline surface in the U parametric direction.

parameters.name: orderv
parameters.label: V Order
parameters.description: If the input is a surface, this is the order of the fitted Spline surface in the V parametric direction. The V order is irrelevant if the input is a face.

parameters.name: tol
parameters.label: Tolerance
parameters.description: This is the primary precision factor in approximation fitting. The smaller the tolerance, the closer the fit and the higher the number of generated vertices. If a small tolerance causes unwanted twist

parameters.name: smooth
parameters.label: Smoothness
parameters.description: For a set tolerance, the smoothness factor allows for more or less roundness in the generated shape. If this parameter is zero, it does not mean that the fit will be sharp. It simply indicates that no

parameters.name: multipleu
parameters.label: U Multiple Knots
parameters.description: Sometimes the data set has sharp bends that must be preserved in the fitted shape. In this case, inserting multiple knots in the areas of sharp curvature will usually produce the right effect. Sometim

parameters.name: multiplev
parameters.label: V Multiple Knots
parameters.description: Sometimes the data set has sharp bends that must be preserved in the fitted shape. In this case, inserting multiple knots in the areas of sharp curvature will usually produce the right effect. Sometim

parameters.name: dataparmu
parameters.label: U Data Parameter
parameters.description: Specifies the parameterization of the data in the U direction (the only direction if the input is a curve). The data parameterization can be uniform, chord length, or centripetal.

parameters.name: dataparmv
parameters.label: V Data Parameter
parameters.description: V data parameterization is identical to U data parameterization, but it affects the V direction when the input is a surface. It is not used when the input is a face.

parameters.name: closeu
parameters.label: U Wrap
parameters.description: This menu determines whether the fitted curve should be closed, or whether the fitted surface should be wrapped in the U parametric direction. The options are to open (Off), close (On), or inherit the

parameters.name: closev
parameters.label: V Wrap
parameters.description: This menu determines whether the fitted surface should be wrapped in the V parametric direction. The options are to open (Off), close (On), or inherit the closure type from the input primitive. V Wrap

parameters.name: corners
parameters.label: Fit Corners
parameters.description: Specifies whether corners in the data should be preserved when doing local curve interpolation.
